evals.expected_output: Full weekly review: discovers accounts with open opps, processes each via subagents, reports results, runs retro
expectations: Config read and vault/Salesforce CLI verified
All account directories scanned for salesforce_opportunity fields
Accounts categorized: active (set up), needs setup, bad opp URL
Bad opp URLs auto-fixed by querying Salesforce for open opps on the Account ID
Account URLs in salesforce_opportunity fields moved to salesforce_account
Subagents launched in parallel for all active accounts
Each subagent pulls deal context via Salesforce REST API
Closed opportunities detected and salesforce_opportunity fields cleared
Unsummarized meetings with transcripts/briefs auto-summarized via /ld-summarize-account
Weekly (status) ledger entry added or updated (only one per account)
Salesforce Updates section updated with status line at top of code block
SE status pushed to Salesforce via PATCH REST API for each open opportunity
Report table shows account, stage, amount, close date, forecast, status entry, SF push
Accounts needing setup listed with prompt to create them
Phase 4 retro: model performance logged, cross-account patterns discovered
Portfolio insights added to Pending Review with [portfolio] tag
Learnings surfaced in tomorrow's daily note if pending items exist

evals.expected_output: Detects closed opportunities, clears frontmatter fields, skips remaining steps for that account
expectations: Subagent queries Salesforce and finds IsClosed=true for all opportunities
salesforce_opportunity fields cleared in account frontmatter
Account reported as 'no_open_opps' status
Remaining steps (ledger, SF push) skipped for this account
Closed opportunity listed in Newly Closed Opportunities report section

evals.expected_output: Auto-summarizes meetings that have transcripts but no summary before continuing
expectations: Meeting files scanned for empty ## Summary sections
Meetings with External Summary or Transcript content identified as summarizable
/ld-summarize-account run for the account before adding ledger entry
Account file and Ledger.md re-read after summarization
Report shows which accounts were auto-summarized and how many meetings

evals.expected_output: Only one (status) entry exists per account in the ledger
expectations: Existing (status) entry in ledger found and replaced (not duplicated)
New (status) entry placed at top of ledger if none existed
Status entry format: '- M/D INITIALS (status): concise update'
Status entry is max ~20 words, includes stage and what's next
No deal amounts, close dates, or forecast in status entry

evals.prompt: /ld-weekly (bad opp URL auto-fix: salesforce_opportunity contains Account URL)
evals.expected_output: Detects Account URL in opportunity field, queries Salesforce for open opps, fixes frontmatter
expectations: salesforce_opportunity field containing /Account/ URL detected as bad opp URL
Account ID extracted from the URL
Salesforce queried: SELECT Id, Name FROM Opportunity WHERE AccountId AND IsClosed = false
If one open opp found: salesforce_opportunity updated with correct Opportunity URL
If multiple open opps found: primary set to first, extras added as salesforce_opportunity_2, etc.
If no open opps found: salesforce_opportunity cleared
Bad Account URL moved to salesforce_account if that field was empty
Account re-categorized after fix

evals.expected_output: Each open opportunity scored as Green/Yellow/Red with justification, pushed to Salesforce if configured
expectations: Each open opportunity evaluated across multiple signals: last meeting date, champion engagement, MEDDPICC completeness, stage velocity, close date movement, stakeholder breadth, technical validation, competitive pressure, next steps clarity
Score is Green (progressing well), Yellow (1-2 risk signals), or Red (multiple risk signals)
One-line justification written (max ~15 words)
Health color appended to ledger status entry as emoji (green/yellow/red circle)
If salesforce_deal_health_field configured: PATCH sent to Salesforce for each open opp
Deal Risk Radar section in report shows Red first, then Yellow, then Green

evals.expected_output: Self-review of run quality, cross-account pattern discovery, and daily note surfacing
expectations: ld-config.md Model Performance table updated with subagent success/failure rates
Recurring Issues section updated with date-stamped observations
Cross-account patterns analyzed: common competitors, objections, tech stacks, deal velocity
Portfolio insights logged under Discovered Patterns > Pending Review with [portfolio] tag
Tomorrow's daily note checked; if exists, task added to review skill learnings
If daily note doesn't exist, pending_daily_surface flag set in learnings file
Retro summary included in final report with stale accounts listed

evals.prompt: /ld-weekly (account with meetings needing input vs summarizable)
evals.expected_output: Distinguishes between meetings that can be auto-summarized and those truly needing user input
expectations: Meetings with External Summary or Transcript but no Summary categorized as 'summarizable'
Meetings with NO External Summary AND no Transcript categorized as 'needs input'
Summarizable meetings auto-processed via /ld-summarize-account
Needs-input meetings listed in report with filenames but processing continues
Account not blocked by needs-input meetings; ledger and SF push still happen
